twitter_thread: IBM says 65% of AI budgets are cannibalizing software budgets. That's $650k from a $1M budget.
Companies like Lyft are using TensorFlow to cut AI costs by 40% and free up software funds.
Google Cloud AI Platform is handling 75% of named entity recognition tasks for clients like Airbus.
Don't underestimate data prep: 80% of a typical AI project's 1,000 hours is spent on data labeling.
Labelbox annotation tools can trim data prep time by 30%, saving 300 hours per project.
What's the most significant challenge you've faced when implementing AI in your organization?
Have you had to cut software features to fund AI initiatives? #aiadoption #softwarebudgets
pack.linkedin: IBM reports that 65% of AI budgets are being reallocated from software budgets, totaling $650k from a $1M budget. To offset this, companies like Lyft are leveraging open-source AI tools like TensorFlow to reduce costs by 40%. Others, such as Airbus, are offloading tasks to cloud services like Google Cloud AI Platform. Implementing AI requires careful navigation of budget trade-offs, particularly when it comes to data preparation. What specific challenges have you faced when allocating resources between AI and software development, and how have you addressed them?
tiktok_hooks: AI is devouring 65% of software budgets
Lyft saved 40% on AI costs with one simple switch
Airbus offloaded 75% of its entity recognition tasks to the cloud
pack.reddit_headline: Is the AI boom quietly killing software innovation with budget cuts?
